name: SWAP 200 Mile Plan (16 weeks)
author: David Roche & Megan Roche, MD PhD
description: Bundled training plan imported from SWAP_Plans PDF templates.

sessions.pace_description: rest
sessions.notes: rest

sessions.pace_description: 4-8 miles easy plus 6 x 20 second hills
sessions.notes: 4-8 miles easy plus 6 x 20 second hills. On hill strides, run smooth and fast up a moderate grade, building neuromuscular efficiency and power. On easy running, think relaxed, effortless flow!

sessions.pace_description: 2-3 miles easy warm-up, 6 x 2 min hills at 5k effort, 4 x 1
sessions.notes: 2-3 miles easy warm-up, 6 x 2 min hills at 5k effort, 4 x 1 min hills at 5k effort, 2-3 miles easy. Muscle power is the first focus! PM: Optional 20-40 min treadhill—10-15% grade hike/run. Anytime “treadhill” is noted, you can run or x-train instead if no treadmill access (all doubles 20- 40 min unless otherwise noted) Mountain legs

sessions.pace_description: 4-8 miles easy or optional x- train
sessions.notes: 4-8 miles easy or optional x- train. Can sub in x-train on any day as things build—for 200s, it’s much less important to focus on running economy, but we still want to promote long- term growth. Be very flexible based on how you feel! Speed legs

sessions.pace_description: Rest or 3-6 miles easy or x-train
sessions.notes: Rest or 3-6 miles easy or x-train. Fridays are flex recovery days to prepare for the weekend!

sessions.pace_description: 10-14 miles easy
sessions.notes: 10-14 miles easy. Ideally long runs are on fun trails, and it’s even better if you have some vert for musculoskeletal strength! Mountain legs

sessions.pace_description: 6-10 miles easy plus 6 x 20 second hills
sessions.notes: 6-10 miles easy plus 6 x 20 second hills. Hill strides to end the week are a strong neuromuscular and mechanical stress! Speed legs

sessions.notes: rest

sessions.notes: 5-10 miles easy

sessions.pace_description: Rest or 3-6 miles easy or x-train
sessions.notes: Rest or 3-6 miles easy or x-train

sessions.pace_description: 12-16 miles easy (15 min mod/hard around 1-hour effort
sessions.notes: 12-16 miles easy (15 min mod/hard around 1-hour effort after warm-up). The tempo will improve aerobic power and lactate shuttling, which will help future workouts! Mountain legs

sessions.pace_description: 8-12 miles easy plus 6 x 20 second hills Speed legs
sessions.notes: 8-12 miles easy plus 6 x 20 second hills Speed legs

sessions.pace_description: rest
sessions.notes: rest

sessions.pace_description: 4-8 miles easy plus 4 x 30 second hills
sessions.notes: 4-8 miles easy plus 4 x 30 second hills

sessions.pace_description: 6-10 miles easy
sessions.notes: 6-10 miles easy. Aerobic week to absorb the stress and recover for things ramping up No double Mountain legs

sessions.pace_description: 4-8 miles easy plus 6 x 20 second hills No speed legs
sessions.notes: 4-8 miles easy plus 6 x 20 second hills No speed legs

sessions.pace_description: Rest or 3-6 miles easy or x-train
sessions.notes: Rest or 3-6 miles easy or x-train

sessions.pace_description: 14-18 miles easy with as much vert as you can
sessions.notes: 14-18 miles easy with as much vert as you can. While all long runs ideally have some vert, this one would be ideal to maximize that stimulus

sessions.pace_description: 4-8 hour hike, with the option to run some downs Speed legs
sessions.notes: 4-8 hour hike, with the option to run some downs Speed legs. This is a great time to increase weight in the exercises!

sessions.pace_description: rest
sessions.notes: rest

sessions.pace_description: 5-10 miles easy with 5 x 20 seconds fast/2 min easy
sessions.notes: 5-10 miles easy with 5 x 20 seconds fast/2 min easy. On these flat strides, think smooth and strong, as fast as you can go without sprinting

sessions.pace_description: 2-3 miles easy warm-up,
sessions.notes: 2-3 miles easy warm-up,

sessions.pace_description: 5-10 miles easy Optional easy double—a relaxed jog or hike
sessions.notes: 5-10 miles easy Optional easy double—a relaxed jog or hike. Fast hikes are great training too! Speed legs

sessions.pace_description: Rest or 3-6 miles easy or x-train
sessions.notes: Rest or 3-6 miles easy or x-train

sessions.pace_description: 12-16 miles easy (20 min mod/hard around 1-hour effort
sessions.notes: 12-16 miles easy (20 min mod/hard around 1-hour effort after warm-up) Mountain legs

sessions.pace_description: 6-12 miles easy with strong downhills Speed legs
sessions.notes: 6-12 miles easy with strong downhills Speed legs

sessions.notes: rest

sessions.pace_description: 5-10 miles easy plus 5 x 20 second hills
sessions.notes: 5-10 miles easy plus 5 x 20 second hills

sessions.pace_description: 2-3 miles easy, 4 x 5 min mod/hard around 10k effort with 2
sessions.notes: 2-3 miles easy, 4 x 5 min mod/hard around 10k effort with 2 min easy recovery, 4 min easy, 4 x 1 min hills around 5k effort, 2 miles easy. These first longer intervals may feel weird at first, don’t overdo it! Building this speed will make all paces easier on the body later. Optional treadhill or double Mountain legs

sessions.notes: 5-12 miles easy Optional easy double Speed legs

sessions.notes: Rest or 4-8 miles easy or x-train

sessions.pace_description: 16-20 miles easy/mod
sessions.notes: 16-20 miles easy/mod. On easy/mod long runs, you can find flow and push a bit if the body feels good Mountain legs

sessions.notes: 8-12 miles easy plus 6 x 20 second hills Speed legs

sessions.pace_description: 14-18 miles easy
sessions.notes: 14-18 miles easy. Aim for a relaxed aerobic day!

sessions.pace_description: 4-8 hour hike, with option to run some downs Speed legs to
sessions.notes: 4-8 hour hike, with option to run some downs Speed legs to end the week

sessions.pace_description: 5-10 miles easy with 5 x 20 seconds fast/2 min easy
sessions.notes: 5-10 miles easy with 5 x 20 seconds fast/2 min easy

sessions.pace_description: 2-3 miles easy, 3 x 8 min around 1-hour effort with 3 min
sessions.notes: 2-3 miles easy, 3 x 8 min around 1-hour effort with 3 min easy recovery, 6 x 1 min around 10k effort with 30 sec easy recovery, 2 miles easy. Working on threshold now, which relies on previous workouts, and will fuel future workouts! It’s all building on itself woohoo! Optional treadhill or double with 2nd half faster Mountain legs

sessions.pace_description: 5-12 miles easy Optional easy double Speed legs
sessions.notes: 5-12 miles easy Optional easy double Speed legs

sessions.pace_description: Rest or 4-8 miles easy or x-train
sessions.notes: Rest or 4-8 miles easy or x-train

sessions.pace_description: 18-22 miles easy/mod (30 min mod/hard around 1-hour effort
sessions.notes: 18-22 miles easy/mod (30 min mod/hard around 1-hour effort after warm-up). This type of run will play much longer than it looks with the tempo, which allows us to get more endurance without overdoing the volume. No strength after long runs now as they get harder!

sessions.pace_description: 10-14 miles easy with strong downs plus 6 x 30 second steep
sessions.notes: 10-14 miles easy with strong downs plus 6 x 30 second steep hills Speed legs

sessions.pace_description: rest
sessions.notes: rest

sessions.pace_description: 2-3 miles easy, 15
sessions.notes: 2-3 miles easy, 15

sessions.pace_description: 4-10 miles easy
sessions.notes: 4-10 miles easy. Two workouts this week!

sessions.pace_description: 6-12 miles easy/mod, can progress to steady or moderate if
sessions.notes: 6-12 miles easy/mod, can progress to steady or moderate if feeling good Optional easy double No strength mid-week with 2 workouts!

sessions.pace_description: Rest or 3-6 miles easy or x-train
sessions.notes: Rest or 3-6 miles easy or x-train

sessions.pace_description: 16-20 miles easy on trails with as much vert as you can
sessions.notes: 16-20 miles easy on trails with as much vert as you can. Prepare the legs for the downhill strain!

sessions.pace_description: 5-10 hour hike, with option to run some downs
sessions.notes: 5-10 hour hike, with option to run some downs. The longer the better on this one! Speed legs

sessions.notes: rest

sessions.pace_description: 5-10 miles easy plus 5 x 20 second hills
sessions.notes: 5-10 miles easy plus 5 x 20 second hills

sessions.pace_description: 2-3 miles easy, 10 x 3 min at 1-hour effort with 1 min easy
sessions.notes: 2-3 miles easy, 10 x 3 min at 1-hour effort with 1 min easy recovery, 2 miles easy. Really smooth intervals! This is a good bread-and-butter threshold stimulus! Optional treadhill or double with 2nd half faster

sessions.notes: 20-25 miles easy/mod on trails (30 min mod/hard around 1- hour effort after the warm-up)

sessions.pace_description: 16-20 miles easy on trails with strong downs No strength
sessions.notes: 16-20 miles easy on trails with strong downs No strength this weekend with increased running volume!

sessions.notes: Rest or 3-6 miles easy or x-train

sessions.pace_description: 10-15 miles easy
sessions.notes: 10-15 miles easy. Very relaxed aerobic weekend Mountain legs after lighter long run

sessions.pace_description: 6-10 miles easy plus 4 x 30 second hills Speed legs
sessions.notes: 6-10 miles easy plus 4 x 30 second hills Speed legs

sessions.pace_description: rest
sessions.notes: rest

sessions.pace_description: 5-12 miles easy

sessions.pace_description: 2-3 miles easy, 3 x 10 min at 1-hour effort with 3 min easy
sessions.notes: 2-3 miles easy, 3 x 10 min at 1-hour effort with 3 min easy recovery, 5 min easy, 5 x 30 second hills fast, 2 miles easy. Final big threshold workout! You rocked this cycle! Optional treadhill or double Mountain legs

sessions.pace_description: 5-12 miles easy Optional easy double No speed legs this
sessions.notes: 5-12 miles easy Optional easy double No speed legs this week with race simulation on weekend.

sessions.pace_description: Rest or 3-6 miles easy or x-train
sessions.notes: Rest or 3-6 miles easy or x-train

sessions.pace_description: 50k moderate
sessions.notes: 50k moderate. 5 weeks out, view this almost like a shorter race—run in a focused way, aiming to get to the end fatigued. An advanced athlete could do a training race like a 50 miler or even 100k!

sessions.pace_description: 5-10 hour hike, with option to run some downs
sessions.notes: 5-10 hour hike, with option to run some downs. Move efficiently, but with little strain or resistance. Rest or do a short hike this day if a 50 miler or 100k the day before!

sessions.pace_description: rest
sessions.notes: rest

sessions.pace_description: 4-10 miles easy plus 5 x 20 second hills
sessions.notes: 4-10 miles easy plus 5 x 20 second hills. Rest or hike on Tues and Wed if 50 miler or 100k!

sessions.pace_description: 2-3 miles easy,
sessions.notes: 2-3 miles easy,

sessions.pace_description: 6-12 miles easy Optional easy double Speed legs Keep it as
sessions.notes: 6-12 miles easy Optional easy double Speed legs Keep it as a short, easy jog if you went longer last week.

sessions.pace_description: Rest or 3-6 miles easy or x-train
sessions.notes: Rest or 3-6 miles easy or x-train

sessions.pace_description: 12-16 miles easy/mod (1 hour moderate after warm-up around
sessions.notes: 12-16 miles easy/mod (1 hour moderate after warm-up around marathon effort). This final long tempo will prepare the aerobic system for the final push next week Mountain legs

sessions.pace_description: 10 miles easy plus 6 x 20 second hills Speed legs
sessions.notes: 10 miles easy plus 6 x 20 second hills Speed legs

sessions.pace_description: rest
sessions.notes: rest

sessions.pace_description: 6-10 miles easy with 5 x 20 seconds fast/2 min easy
sessions.notes: 6-10 miles easy with 5 x 20 seconds fast/2 min easy

sessions.pace_description: 6-12 miles easy/mod, progressing to steady or moderate if
sessions.notes: 6-12 miles easy/mod, progressing to steady or moderate if feeling good. Another one where you might see big progress! No double Speed legs

sessions.pace_description: 6-12 miles easy
sessions.notes: 6-12 miles easy

sessions.pace_description: Rest or 4-8 miles easy or x-train
sessions.notes: Rest or 4-8 miles easy or x-train

sessions.pace_description: 25-30 miles easy on trails, aiming to finish feeling good
sessions.notes: 25-30 miles easy on trails, aiming to finish feeling good. Fuel like in the 200!

sessions.pace_description: 20-25 miles easy on trails with strong downs
sessions.notes: 20-25 miles easy on trails with strong downs. Work on purposeful flow on downhills on tired legs No strength with higher running volume weekend

sessions.pace_description: rest
sessions.notes: rest

sessions.pace_description: 5-8 miles easy plus 4 x 20 second hills
sessions.notes: 5-8 miles easy plus 4 x 20 second hills

sessions.pace_description: 2-3 miles easy, 30 min
sessions.notes: 2-3 miles easy, 30 min

sessions.pace_description: 5-8 miles easy Speed legs
sessions.notes: 5-8 miles easy Speed legs. Final strength session!

sessions.pace_description: Rest or 3-6 miles easy or x-train
sessions.notes: Rest or 3-6 miles easy or x-train

sessions.pace_description: 14-18 miles easy/mod on trails with strong downhills
sessions.notes: 14-18 miles easy/mod on trails with strong downhills. 2 weeks out from the race is the last time we want to risk any muscle damage, so find some fun trails and push the downs a bit for one last stimulus for the eccentric muscle contractions and impact

sessions.pace_description: 8-14 miles easy run/hike
sessions.notes: 8-14 miles easy run/hike. Ideally around race effort—getting a feel for how you are going to approach the day.

sessions.pace_description: rest
sessions.notes: rest

sessions.pace_description: 4-8 miles easy
sessions.notes: 4-8 miles easy

sessions.pace_description: 5-8 miles easy/mod, progressing to steady or moderate if
sessions.notes: 5-8 miles easy/mod, progressing to steady or moderate if feeling good

sessions.pace_description: 4-6 miles easy
sessions.notes: 4-6 miles easy

sessions.pace_description: Rest
sessions.notes: Rest. Dialing things back!

sessions.pace_description: 8-12 miles easy
sessions.notes: 8-12 miles easy. The final long run goal is to find some flow on fun trails, working on a positive and loving self-talk that will be key during the race

sessions.pace_description: 2-4 hour hike
sessions.notes: 2-4 hour hike. Time on feet, dialing in aerobic metabolism for race day

sessions.pace_description: rest
sessions.notes: rest

sessions.pace_description: 4-6 miles easy on
sessions.notes: 4-6 miles easy on

sessions.pace_description: 2-4 miles easy
sessions.notes: 2-4 miles easy

sessions.pace_description: Rest and recovery
sessions.notes: Rest and recovery. This is getting real!

sessions.pace_description: 2-3 miles very easy in AM
sessions.notes: 2-3 miles very easy in AM. Shake out the nerves and the bowels, preparing emotionally for a loving adventure into the unknown.

sessions.pace_description: Race celebration of life!
sessions.notes: Race celebration of life!

sessions.pace_description: YOU GOT THIS!!!
sessions.notes: YOU GOT THIS!!!
